description: fetchPaymentAuthorization sets up AbortController + setTimeout to enforce a 2s deadline; fetchUserProfile and fetchCmsBanner trust the upstream service to time out on its own. Unifying onto a shared fetcher that drops the AbortController re-introduces head-of-line blocking on the checkout path.

findings.rationale: fetchPaymentAuthorization has explicit AbortController + setTimeout + try/finally cleanup that the other two fetchers omit. The setup is load-bearing on the checkout path: a degraded upstream without the abort blocks workers indefinitely. Structural-unclassified or label-divergence territory — the body is multi-line and complex.
